final_requirements.title: Interpret all-caps BCP 14 keywords

Acknowledge ack-eliciting packets promptly
Define packets in flight
Attach packet-level headers to transmissions

Carry packet numbers in packet headers
Map encryption level to packet number space
Prohibit packet number reuse
Send packet numbers monotonically
Allow intentional packet number gaps
Permit mixed frame types per packet
Ensure reliable delivery outcome
Allow retransmission in new packets
Acknowledge all packets
Delay acknowledgment for non-ack-eliciting packets
Shorten CRYPTO acknowledgment timers
Count non-ACK packets toward congestion limits
Treat non-ACK packets as in flight
Count PADDING toward bytes in flight
Suppress direct ACKs for PADDING
Compute RTT path values
Gate RTT sample generation
Measure latest RTT from the largest acknowledged packet
Use only the largest acknowledged packet
Skip duplicate RTT updates
Require ack-eliciting progress
Initialize min_rtt from the first sample

Avoid over-refreshing min_rtt

Ignore max_ack_delay before handshake confirmation
Allow dropping some adjusted samples
Subtract local decryption delay

Reset the estimator on the first post-init sample

Update RTT estimates on subsequent samples

Bound adjusted RTT by min_rtt
Separate loss detection by packet number space
Declare loss only for packets that satisfy the basic loss criteria
Allow smaller initial reordering thresholds with adaptation
Recommend a packet threshold of three

Declare earlier packets lost after sufficient time
Bound the time threshold by timer granularity

Use the recommended packet-threshold multiplier

Allow alternative time-threshold experiments
Send probe datagrams on PTO expiration or address-validation uncertainty
Compute PTO per packet number space
Do not infer loss from PTO expiration
Schedule PTO after ack-eliciting transmission
Set max_ack_delay to zero for early handshake spaces
Keep PTO above granularity
Use the earlier PTO across Initial and Handshake spaces
Defer application-data PTO until handshake confirmation
Restart PTO on send, acknowledgment, or key discard

Reuse prior-smoothed RTT on resumed connections
Default initial RTT to 333 milliseconds
Use PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE timing for initial RTT
Do not treat PATH_CHALLENGE/PATH_RESPONSE delay as an RTT sample
Reset timers when keys are discarded

Reset the server PTO when the client sends data
Fire a past-due PTO immediately
Arm the client PTO before handshake confirmation
Send Handshake probes when keys are available
Send Initial probes otherwise
Permit early CRYPTO probes for handshake speedup
Probe with at least one ack-eliciting packet
Allow two full-sized PTO datagrams
Keep PTO probe packets ack-eliciting
Use other packet number spaces for PTO probes

Allow previously sent data in PTO probes
Allow alternative probe-content strategies
Send a PING when no probe data exists
Allow declaring in-flight packets lost instead of probing

Allow using Retry-derived RTT as the initial RTT

Discard recovery state for rejected 0-RTT packets
Discard secrets as soon as the replacement keys exist
Require alternate controllers to obey RFC 8085
Exclude ACK-only packets from bytes in flight

Allow ACK-only loss signals to influence control
Keep congestion control per path
Respect the bytes-in-flight ceiling
Treat ECN CE as congestion on validated paths
Start each connection in slow start
Recommend the initial congestion window
Recompute the initial window when datagram size changes
Reset the initial window after handshake-driven MTU reduction

Enter recovery on loss or ECN-CE increase

Stay in recovery once entered
Do not reenter recovery while already there

Restore congestion window before leaving recovery

Hold the congestion window steady during recovery

Do not ignore later packet loss
Do not block probe packets with congestion control
Count probe packets as additional flight
Declare persistent congestion when all long-duration packets are lost

Establish persistent congestion after the full loss test passes

Update RTT only for newly acknowledged ack-eliciting packets
Process ECN and loss state on acknowledgment

Initialize RTT estimation on the first sample

Start PTO timing from now when no ack-eliciting packets are in flight

Include max_ack_delay and backoff in Application Data PTO
Complete address validation for servers and validated clients
Set or cancel the loss detection timer according to loss and PTO state
Handle timeout-driven loss detection first
